mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending capacity, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the negative predictive relationship vanishes when UNRATE exceeds 6.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, which boosts corporate revenue forecasts and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS and HOUST diverge in direction, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which triggers short-term momentum buying and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction during inflationary supply shocks, or 5-day forward returns show negative correlation with INDPRO growth when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.5% YoY.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price index levels trigger expectations of aggressive monetary tightening and margin compression, which suppresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive when CPIAUCSL exceeds 200, or the negative predictive relationship disappears when real interest rates are negative.

mechanism_narrative: Growth in total nonfarm payrolls directly increases aggregate wage income and consumer purchasing power, which supports corporate earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 20-day S&P 500 returns across multiple business cycles, or the signal Rank IC drops below 0.01 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter expansion in real gross domestic product confirms macroeconomic growth momentum, which reduces recession risk premiums and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days following data release.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict positive returns when GDPC1 growth decelerates despite remaining positive, or 5-day returns are negative in > 60% of expansion quarters.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising industrial production and falling unemployment indicates a synchronized supply-demand recovery, which strengthens corporate profit margins and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal loses predictive power when supply chain bottlenecks cause production to rise while unemployment remains stagnant, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant in out-of-sample validation.
